bullets.text: Lebanon is entering peace talks with Israel, though the Lebanese government acknowledges it has limited influence over the involved groups.

The wife of Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez, Begoña Gómez, has been charged with corruption following a two-year investigation into allegations she used her relationship to advance her private career.
A treaty to cede sovereignty of the Chagos Islands to Mauritius is now deemed "impossible to agree at a political level" by a UK minister, following the US withdrawal of support.
Australia's largest private cattle feedlot, Mort & Co, is on the market amidst a surge in grain-fed beef demand, with the company putting the majority of its assets up for sale.

Sydney community radio station 2SER faces potential closure in July after over 45 years, following the withdrawal of its main funder, Macquarie University, and a failure to secure alternative financial backing.

Qantas is implementing higher fares and fewer flights in an effort to mitigate the financial impact of increased jet fuel costs, which have risen by hundreds of millions of dollars due to the Iran war.
Oil prices have retreated amid reports of renewed talks between Iran and the US, with President Masoud Pezeshkian indicating Tehran will accept discussions within international law.
Stocks rose and oil declined on optimism for a potential Iran deal, as President Donald Trump signaled a willingness to resume talks, potentially easing Middle East tensions.
The conflict in the Middle East is expected to prolong volatility, with Euroclear Group CEO Valerie Urbain noting it will be more sustained than previous crises.
